title: Meta launches its Muse Image generator and draws criticism for using Instagram photos without permission
summary: Meta's artificial intelligence creates images from public Instagram photos, sparking controversy over privacy and user consent.

title: Massive software updates: Windows 11, Chrome 150, OpenSSH, Proton, and more
summary: Major software platforms release new versions with security, performance, and functionality improvements, including post-quantum support and new drivers.

title: Wave of cyber threats: leaks, banking malware, critical vulnerabilities, and AI attacks
summary: From backdoors in routers to AI scams and Linux flaws, cybersecurity faces a diverse and growing threat landscape.

title: OpenAI introduces GPT-Live, its new voice mode for more natural conversations with ChatGPT
summary: ChatGPT's voice system can now listen and speak simultaneously, improving the fluidity of interactions.

title: OpenAI, MiniMax, DeepSeek, and Nvidia drive the next generation of AI models and chips
summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.6, MiniMax's 2.7 trillion parameter model, DeepSeek's chip, and Nvidia's Vera CPU set the pace for AI innovation.

title: Sony will delete PlayStation digital games after three years of inactivity and faces lawsuits and stock drop
summary: The new policy of deleting digital games triggers a million-dollar lawsuit, CEO stock sale, and PS Plus discounts to retain subscribers.

title: Apple to invest $30 billion in Broadcom chips manufactured in the United States
summary: Apple expands its agreement with Broadcom to produce wireless chips in the US, bolstering domestic manufacturing.

title: Meta's smart glasses raise alarm over continuous recording and lack of transparency
summary: Meta tests glasses with permanent recording capability, while users and experts warn about privacy risks.

title: Microsoft lays off 4,800 Xbox employees and its console strategy is questioned
summary: The Xbox division suffers massive cuts and criticism from Bernie Sanders, while seeking to grow its player base.

title: Microsoft, Google, and Anthropic launch new tools to create and run AI agents
summary: Flint, Gemini API with background execution, and Claude Cowork on mobile represent big tech's bet on autonomous agents.

title: Launch of the first commercial nuclear satellite and multi-billion dollar investments in the space industry
summary: SpaceX launches a nuclear satellite, Blue Origin and Venus Aerospace raise large funds, and the Space Force debuts a microwave weapon.

title: New genetic research links obesity to heredity and microplastics to health risks
summary: Recent studies reveal that obesity is largely genetic, microplastics accumulate in the body, and creatine could help against cancer.

title: Hardware news: new standards, CPUs, GPUs, and component deals
summary: Intel, AMD, Nvidia, and other manufacturers launch new processors, graphics cards, and memory standards that boost PC performance.

title: Backdoor found in Tenda routers that allows passwordless access, and the company does not fix it
summary: Several Tenda router models contain a critical unpatched vulnerability that grants administrative access without authentication.

title: DLSS Swapper tool could contain malware if third-party DLL files are downloaded
summary: The mod creator warns that user-uploaded DLL files can infect the PC, urging to use only official sources.

title: Mistral introduces Robostral Navigate, an AI model for robotic navigation with a single camera
summary: The 8 billion parameter model allows robots to orient themselves with one camera, marking Mistral's entry into robotics.

title: Oregon raises electricity rate for data centers by 30% and reduces it by 1.3% for households
summary: The POWER Act forces large consumers to pay more for electricity, while residents get minimal relief.

title: Grok 4.5, Fable 5, and GPT 5.5 compete in performance and price, narrowing benchmark differences
summary: Next-generation AI models show that price is increasingly decisive compared to small performance differences.

title: Multiple vulnerabilities in GitHub affect verified commits, Copilot, and repository privacy
summary: Researchers demonstrate that verified commits can be rewritten without breaking the signature, Copilot ignores restrictions, and AI agents leak private repos.

title: Climate change causes permanent tropical nights in the Mediterranean and limits mobility
summary: New data shows that tropical nights are now the norm, extreme heat confines people, and North American emissions grow.
